summary: McKenzie documents his iterative process of redesigning download buttons for Bingo Card Creator, focusing on pixel-perfect alignment and adding explanatory text for non-technical users. He describes spending significant time on per-pixel adjustments to ensure visual consistency between button variants. The post captures the obsessive attention to detail required when optimizing conversion elements for unsophisticated users. He also candidly shares mistakes made during the process, including a flopped layout he didn't catch initially. The post illustrates how seemingly trivial UI elements can consume disproportionate effort when precision matters for conversions.
key_points: Adding redundant explanatory text to buttons helps non-technical users understand clickable elements
Pixel-perfect alignment between button variants requires disproportionate effort but matters for professional appearance
Using the right tools (layers in image editors) dramatically reduces time spent on precise visual composition
Small UI details like an embossed cursor icon on a button will attract a significant percentage of clicks
money_quotes: When your customers are not the technically sharpest knives in the drawer, sometimes I think a little redundancy can be a good thing.
I guarantee you 25% of the clicks on the button will hit the cursor.
When you need images to line up perfectly, you need layers.
Eight pixels is hard to see!
I sometimes get tunnel vision for little details.
key_insight: Optimizing conversion elements for non-technical users requires obsessive attention to visual details and redundant cues that technical people would consider unnecessary.
